summary: Fun, hot zoo show captured in an unknown‑source recording, highlighted by a sweet "They Love Each Other" and a joyous "Playing In The Band" sequence.
review: The reviewer paints a vivid picture of a laid‑back, midsummer gig at the Oklahoma City Zoo, noting strong vocal performances from Bob and a particularly sweet rendition of "They Love Each Other." Highlights include an exuberant "Playing In The Band" opener, a beloved China > Rider close, and a soulful stretch through Lost Sailor to Black Peter. No recording‑quality details are given, so the tape is assumed to be listenable but uncharacterized.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 4‑star review praises many song performances and overall vibe, suggesting a good show; lack of recording comments keeps confidence at medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer does not mention soundboard vs. audience or any clarity issues; no specific audio quality commentary provided.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed, sunny afternoon set that moves smoothly from early‑day classics into a high‑energy second set; heat made the Drums > Space segment feel a bit blurred but overall the pacing kept the crowd dancing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his sweet lead on "They Love Each Other" and gentle transition into Black Peter.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for fine vocal deliveries on "C.C. Rider", "Me & My Uncle" and "Big River".
band_member_comments.Drums: Heat caused the Drums > Space segment to feel blurred, though no major complaints were voiced.
